expectations.text: The reference card names code-simplifier as a distinct, required Phase 6 subagent step.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 1 — Launch code-simplifier subagent (always required, runs first)' — named as a discrete step, marked required, with workflow language quoted: 'Always launch an independent code-simplifier subagent to identify refactoring opportunities after implementation.'

expectations.text: The reference card names code-reviewer as a distinct, required Phase 6 subagent step.
expectations.evidence: 'Step 2 — Launch code-reviewer agent(s) (always required, runs after Step 1)' with workflow language: 'Always run independent code-reviewer agents for quality review on every track.' Track-specific minimums table included.

expectations.text: The reference card shows code-simplifier executed before code-reviewer (not after, not as a merged step).
expectations.evidence: Sequence diagram: '[code-simplifier] ← Step 1: always, first → [code-reviewer(s)] ← Step 2: always, after simplifier'. Step markers '(always required, runs first)' and '(always required, runs after Step 1)' make order explicit. Non-negotiable constraint 2: 'code-simplifier always runs before code-reviewer — the order is not flexible.'

expectations.text: The card does not describe Phase 6 as exclusively code-reviewer agents with no simplifier step.
expectations.evidence: code-simplifier is prominently Step 1. Non-negotiable constraint 1: 'code-simplifier and code-reviewer are separate, independent subagents — they cannot be merged into a single combined pass.'

expectations.text: The card attributes these steps to the workflow's Phase 6 specification, not personal preference.
expectations.evidence: Card header: 'Source: feature-dev workflow, Phase 6 specification'. Footer: 'This card describes only what Phase 6 of the feature-dev workflow specifies. No steps have been added from general engineering practice.' Workflow language is quoted for each step.
